exceptions.reason: The host's role binding is `tone() === 'danger' ? 'alert' : 'status'` -- role is only "alert" when tone is danger. Every other tone (info, success, warning, neutral -- including the default) renders role="status" instead, the same more-polite live region React's Alert.jsx falls back to for the identical set of tones. This binding stays on alert because it is the safety-relevant contract, the one that matters when Alert is carrying a condition the user must not miss; the exception records that only the danger tone actually earns it.
